English
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Gwen J. Bymers papers
Bymers, Gwen J., 1915-
23-18-1787
4.1 cubic feet. 4.1 cubic feet.
[ca.1930]-2002. Correspondence, course materials. Includes correspondence, 1960-1976; historical material, ca.1935-1973; and other miscellaneous papers reflecting Bymers' career as a professor of consumer economics. Collection material in English 2074771
General
Contact Information:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu
Compiled by:
Carolyn Smith
Date completed:
Feb. 16, 2004
EAD encoding:
Peter Martinez, Mar. 9, 2004
Date modified:
RMC Staff, October 2018
BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
Professor emeritus of consumer economics, Cornell University.
Gwen Bymers was born on June 15, 1915. During World War II she joined the Women's Army Corp and after the war, through the GI Bill, obtained her B.A. degree from the University of North Dakota in 1946 and her Ph.D. in economics from U.C.L.A. in 1958. In 1956 Bymers came to Cornell to become an assistant professor in the Dept. of Household Economics and Management. In 1969 she became chair of the newly formed Dept. of Consumer Economics and Public Policy, a position she held for seven years. Gwen Bymers retired in 1977.
COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
Includes correspondence, 1960-1976; historical material, ca.1935-1973; publications, photographs, and other papers reflecting Bymers' career as a professor of consumer economics and her work in Ghana.
SERIES LIST
Series I. Correspondence
Boxes 1, 2 and 5
Series II. Department and Course Material
Box 3
Series III. Research and Consumer Education
Box 3
Series IV. Committees, Workshops, and Seminars
Box 4
Series V. Speeches, Talks, and Papers
Box 4
Series VI. Photographs
Box 3, 4 and 5
Series VII. Biographical Material
Box 5
Cite As:
Gwen J. Bymers papers, #23-18-1787. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Related Materials
See also collections #23-2-2817 and #47-10-3394 for additional biographical information.
College teachers. Photographs. Ghana -- Study and teaching. Economics. Consumers. Bymers, Gwen J., 1915- Cornell University -- : Faculty.
